sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Very important findings:
1. Source Y click at (29, 10) did NOT change yellow - it stayed at 5px! So my earlier observation that source Y increments might have been wrong, or the source Y shape position changed.
2. Clicking (38, 24) on the top dest Y shape also did NOT change yellow - stayed at 5px.
So both clicks had no gameplay effect. Yellow is still at 5px.
Wait, let me reconsider. The source Y shape is at node 16 with boundary [[28, 9], [28, 11], [32, 11], [32, 9]]. So (29, 10) should be inside the source Y shape. But it didn't change anything.
Hmm, but earlier I thought clicking the source Y shape at (29, 10) changed yellow from 5 to 14. Let me check if that was actually the case. Looking back at the previous output:
"After source Y click: Y={16: 5, 20: 7, 23: 7}" - yellow stayed at 5px!
Wait, but before this, the previous turn showed yellow went from 14 to 5 after clicking (40, 24). So the state was yellow=5px before the source Y click. And after the source Y click, yellow was still 5px. So the source Y click did NOT increment yellow.
But earlier I thought it did! Let me re-examine. In the previous turn, I said "Source Y shape click incremented yellow from 5→14!" but looking at the actual data, after clicking (40, 24), yellow went from 14 to 5. Then I clicked source Y at (29, 10) and yellow stayed at 5. So source Y click at (29, 10) had no effect when yellow=5px.
Wait, I need to re-read the previous turn more carefully. The first action was MOUSE(40, 24) which changed yellow from 14 to 5. Then I tried to get to (5, 5) state by clicking dest N shapes twice (23→14→5) and then dest Y shape (23→14) and dest Y frame (14→5). But the output showed yellow was already 5px after the first click.
So the state transitions were:
- Before turn: green=23, yellow=14
- After MOUSE(40, 24): green=23, yellow=5 (not 23 as I expected)
- After MOUSE(21, 40): green=14, yellow=5
- After MOUSE(21, 40): green=5, yellow=5
- After MOUSE(40, 24): green=5, yellow=5 (no change, already at 5)
- After MOUSE(41, 21): green=5, yellow=5 (no change)
So (40, 24) when yellow=14px → yellow=5px. And (40, 24) when yellow=5px → no change. This means (40, 24) decrements yellow by one state.
And now in this turn:
- Before: green=5, yellow=5
- After MOUSE(29, 10): green=5, yellow=5 (no change)
- After MOUSE(38, 24): green=5, yellow=5 (no change)
So source Y shape click and top dest Y shape click both had no effect when yellow=5px. This means the Y toggle only works in certain directions from certain states.
Let me reconsider the toggle mechanics:
- Dest N shape: toggles green between adjacent states (5↔14↔23)
- Dest N frame: sets green to 23
- Dest Y shape: toggles yellow between 14 and 23 (based on early evidence)
- Dest Y frame: decrements yellow 14→5
- (40, 24): decrements yellow 14→5 (this is probably the charcoal separator node 19)
Wait, node 19 is the charcoal box with boundary [[36, 22], [36, 26], [40, 26], [40, 22]]. Position (40, 24) is on the bottom edge of this box. So clicking (40, 24) clicked on the charcoal box node 19, which decrements yellow 14→5.
So the charcoal boxes might be the key! Let me check:
- Node 10: charcoal in green dest, left box [[19, 37], [19, 41], [23, 41], [23, 37]]
- Node 12: charcoal in green dest, right box [[19, 43], [19, 47], [23, 47], [23, 43]]
- Node 19: charcoal in yellow dest, top box [[36, 22], [36, 26], [40, 26], [40, 22]]
- Node 22: charcoal in yellow dest, bottom box [[42, 22], [42, 26], [46, 26], [46, 22]]
I clicked (40, 24) which is on the bottom edge of node 19. This decremented yellow from 14 to 5.
So the charcoal boxes might control the sources! Let me think about what they do:
- Node 19 (top yellow dest charcoal): decrements yellow 14→5
- Node 22 (bottom yellow dest charcoal): might increment yellow or do something else
- Node 10 (left green dest charcoal): might control green
- Node 12 (right green dest charcoal): might control green
Let me test clicking node 22 (bottom yellow dest charcoal) to see if it increments yellow.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Key findings:
# - Source Y click (29,10): no effect when yellow=5
# - Top dest Y click (38,24): no effect when yellow=5
# - (40,24) on charcoal node 19: decremented yellow 14→5
#
# Charcoal boxes might be the real controls!
# Node 19 (top yellow charcoal): decrements yellow 14→5
# Node 22 (bottom yellow charcoal): might increment yellow
# Node 10 (left green charcoal): might control green
# Node 12 (right green charcoal): might control green
#
# Current: green=5, yellow=5
# Let me test clicking charcoal boxes

seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Test node 22 (bottom yellow charcoal) - center at ~[44, 24]
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
y_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
print(f"After node 22 click: N={n_pixels}, Y={y_pixels}")
print("Level completed?", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))

if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    # Test node 10 (left green charcoal) - center at ~[21, 39]
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    y_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
    n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
    print(f"After node 10 click: N={n_pixels}, Y={y_pixels}")
    print("Level completed?", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
    
    if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        # Test node 12 (right green charcoal) - center at ~[21, 45]
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 45}])
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        y_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
        n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
        print(f"After node 12 click: N={n_pixels}, Y={y_pixels}")
        print("Level completed?", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Charcoal boxes are key controls.", "goal_model": "Testing charcoal box effects", "recent_findings": ["Charcoal node 19 decrements yellow 14->5", "Source Y and dest Y clicks had no effect at yellow=5"], "open_questions": ["What do other charcoal boxes do?", "What is the win condition?"], "current_plan": "Test all 4 charcoal boxes", "strategy_log": ["Mapping toggle transitions"], "failed_probes": ["red dot", "charcoal border", "red cross", "separator bar", "small squares", "source Y at yellow=5", "dest Y at yellow=5"]}}
</parameter>
